title: T-012 — Warehouse RLS policies (tenant_id isolation, ADR-003/013)
prompt:
# T-012 — Warehouse RLS policies

PRD: `05-WAREHOUSE-PRD.md` §3, §16.5 (ADR-003/013).
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`.

## Goal
Enable Row-Level Security on every warehouse-owned table with tenant_id matching `current_setting('app.tenant_id')::uuid` per ADR-003/013.

## Implementation contract
Migration `0NN_warehouse_rls.sql` ALTERs each table (license_plates, lp_genealogy, lp_reservations, grns, grn_items, stock_moves, pick_overrides, shelf_life_rules, warehouse_settings) ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY + CREATE POLICY tenant_isolation FOR ALL USING (tenant_id = current_setting('app.tenant_id')::uuid).
Confirm `app.tenant_id` is set in `packages/db/src/connection.ts` per request middleware (assumed present from 00-FOUNDATION).

## Files (scope)
- packages/db/migrations/0NN_warehouse_rls.sql [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/__tests__/warehouse-rls.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given two LPs with different tenant_id, when SELECT runs with `app.tenant_id` set to tenant A, then only tenant A's rows are returned.
2. Given INSERT with mismatched tenant_id (vs session setting), when committed, then DB raises an RLS violation.
3. Given `app.tenant_id` is unset, when SELECT runs against license_plates, then DB raises (NULL cast) error or returns 0 rows depending on policy strictness — confirm the chosen behavior is documented.

## Test strategy
- RED: warehouse-rls.test.ts using two test tenants verifying isolation and forced-deny.
- Run `pnpm --filter db migrate && pnpm --filter db test warehouse-rls`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not skip RLS on any warehouse table.
- Do not use BYPASSRLS roles in production.
- Do not derive tenant from JWT (per ADR-013 users.org_id only).

pipeline_inputs.description: Enable RLS on all warehouse tables and add tenant_isolation policy per ADR-003/013.
pipeline_inputs.details: Migration ALTERs nine warehouse tables ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY + CREATE POLICY tenant_isolation. Tests use two-tenant fixture to assert isolation and forced-deny.
acceptance_criteria: Given two LPs in different tenants, when SELECT runs with app.tenant_id=tenantA, then only tenant A rows are returned
Given INSERT with tenant_id mismatching the session setting, when committed, then DB raises an RLS violation
Given app.tenant_id is unset, when SELECT runs against license_plates, then the DB returns zero rows or a documented NULL-cast error
test_strategy: Run 'pnpm --filter db migrate && pnpm --filter db test warehouse-rls'
risk_red_lines: Do not skip RLS on any warehouse table
Do not use BYPASSRLS roles in production
Do not derive tenant from JWT
